description: Having duty or obligation; being accountable for one's actions and their consequences. 'Thonai' names the weight willingly carried—duty accepted, not imposed.
sound_symbolism: The word bears with acceptance. 'Tho' breathes with thoughtful weight, responsibility recognized. 'Na' settles with grounded acceptance, duty taken up. Then 'i' rises to accountable standing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'thonai miona' (responsible person). Describes one who honors 'seru' (commit) and acts with 'sorai' (insight).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Responsibility as karma—recognizing that actions have consequences, choosing wisely.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Collective responsibility—each person accountable for community and planet.
